focus: RECONCILE observed non-convergence with the code model + start the operator runbook

graphEvents.label: Completed background monitors observed embedder reindex job completion
Persistent context-server starts retry-manager background job
Retry manager claims clean pending rows before embedding
Retention runs before retry queue claims
Retry retention env is frozen at daemon process spawn/module load
retryEmbedding is the reliable backlog success writer after reset

label: Completed success-unchanged monitors match embedder reindex job completion, not retry-manager metadata success writes
Retry-manager background drain runs inside persistent context-server startup, but daemon startup hardcodes 5min/5 batch over batch/interval env
Clean pending rows are retry-manager eligible; claimRetryCandidate moves pending to retry before retryEmbedding writes success
Retention still runs before claims; stale/default env can park pending/retry rows before embedding attempts
Retry env is frozen at daemon module load; MCP reconnect bridges to existing process and cannot reload cap/max-age
Backlog runbook must use retry-manager drain after resetting failed rows; embedder_set is vector-only and cannot converge metadata statuses
Live DB after iteration research: failed=16344, pending=972, retry=10, success=9652
Live failed rows split into 7105 max-age failures and 9239 pending-cap failures

Lease file showed mk-spec-memory launcher pid 49658 started at 2026-05-26T21:21:45.936Z; process listing was unavailable in sandbox because pgrep failed with sysmond service not found
A completed embedder reindex job is not evidence that memory_index.embedding_status rows were updated
Rows already marked failed are outside getRetryQueue selection until reset to pending or retry

direction: Use embedder_set or embedder_status monitor as the metadata backlog drain trigger
reason: The path writes vector/job/active-pointer state but does not update memory_index.embedding_status, so success can remain unchanged after completion.

direction: Use MCP reconnect to apply retry retention env changes
reason: Reconnect bridges to the existing daemon process; retry cap/max-age constants were read at module load in that process.
